translations.### iPlayground 2019 是怎麼樣的體驗？\n\n: ### What was the experience like at iPlayground 2019?
#### **1. [Wei Wang (貓神)](https://medium.com/u/52b3ba2db3a){:target="_blank"}'s Network Request Component Design**
translations.#### 3. [Ethan Huang](https://medium.com: #### 3. [Ethan Huang](https://medium.com/u/e13f6afcf9b9){:target="_blank"} Greatly Developing Apple Watch APP with SwiftUI
translations.#### 4. TinXie-易致及羊小咩兩位大大的 APP安全議題\n\n: #### 4. APP Security Topics by TinXie-易致 and 羊小咩
translations.#### 5. 丁沛堯大大的 優化編譯速度\n\n: #### 5. Pei-Yao Ting's Optimization of Compilation Speed
translations.**吃的及 After Party** ，兩天都是精緻餐盒，冰咖啡、茶飲全天無限:
**Food and After Party**  
Both days featured exquisite boxed meals, with unlimited iced coffee and tea available all day. Last year felt more like a true After Party, like listening to senior developers share stories in a bar, which was very fun. This year was more like afternoon tea (still served alcohol, and the shumai and desserts were delicious!). People mingled on their own, and I actually made new friends this year.
translations.**議程方面** ，除了國內的大大，這次也廣邀國外講者來台分享；其中高朋滿座的絕: **Agenda**: Besides local experts, this event also invited international speakers to share; the most popular was none other than the "Cat God" [Wei Wang](https://medium.com/u/52b3ba2db3a){:target="_blank"}. This year, workshops with hands-on teaching were introduced for the first time, but spots were limited, so you had to act fast… Those busy eating and chatting missed out.
translations.**贊助商攤位、 Ask the Speaker 區** 因場地大交流更方便、更:
**Sponsor Booths and Ask the Speaker Area**  
With the larger venue, networking was easier and there were more activities. From the [iChef](https://www.ichefpos.com/zh-tw){:target="_blank"} booth [#iCHEFxiPlayground](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/ichefxiplayground?source=feed_text&epa=HASHTAG&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAlb4Af_SMM2oWX2M2YI4IDlCbBFp6p-4K1xJC-ywTj7fb1i6EztwESLyMgpJmt86RzJNT1M5CYYaN86LkbHS6JKHUQ2QImFxzem3_8f49wdHBCxV98vW6dy24-XafX22JYEQh8vkdWb-R9vJbKDDjsfMVZ7ONdkks0uIgls9gJVBz66l6p0ytXiq1XpvcCiTHUU5jirEletQZ4wDayw_He9-tmz57NfMKc4QYgdaYFhXWmNNxkkAz3JdVcZlLqaURBNQ&__tn__=%2ANK-R){:target="_blank"}, I got an eco-friendly straw and a dorayaki. At the [Dcard](https://www.dcard.tw/){:target="_blank"} booth, having received stickers and an eco-cup sleeve last year, this year I also got a wet wipe with cynical quotes. Filling out a survey at [17 Live](https://17.live/){:target="_blank"} entered me into a draw for [Airpods 2](../33afa0ae557d/). I picked up stickers at the [weak self](https://weakself.dev/){:target="_blank"} podcast booth. There were also interactive booths from [Grindr](https://www.grindr.com/){:target="_blank"}, [CakeResume](https://www.cakeresume.com/zh-TW){:target="_blank"}, and [Bitrise](https://www.bitrise.io/){:target="_blank"}. Attached is a **partial** photo of my loot.
translations.**首先是場地部分** ，去年在地下一樓會議廳，活動空間不大頗有壓迫感、講座教室: **First, the venue**: Last year, the event was held in a basement conference room, which was small and felt cramped, making it difficult to use computers during lectures. This year, it was moved to the College of Liberal Arts at NTU, which is spacious, new, not crowded, and the classrooms have desks and power outlets, making it convenient to use personal computers!

translations.「垃圾需要分類，代碼也是…」\n\n: "Trash needs sorting, and so does code..."
translations.「當破解成本大於保護成本，APP就是安全的」\n\n: "When the cost of cracking exceeds the cost of protection, the APP is secure."
translations.之前寫過一篇「 [動手做一支 Apple Watch App 吧！](../e8:
I previously wrote an article "[Let's Build an Apple Watch App!](../e85d77b05061/)" based on watchOS 5 using the traditional method; I didn't expect that now we can develop with SwiftUI!  
Apple Watch OS 6 supports generations 1 to 5, so **there are fewer version issues**. Using watch apps to practice SwiftUI is a good starting point (simpler); I'll find time to update it.  
*p.s. I didn't expect watchOS developers to be so niche QQ. I personally find it fun and hope more people will join!*
translations.介紹 iOS ≥ 13 推出的新方法 [UICollectionViewComp:
Introducing the new method [UICollectionViewCompositionalLayout](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uicollectionviewcompositionallayout){:target="_blank"} introduced in iOS ≥ 13, which lets us avoid subclassing UICollectionViewLayout or nesting CollectionView Cells inside CollectionViews to achieve complex layouts as before.  
I can relate to this because our app used the latter approach to achieve the desired design. The peak was nesting a CollectionView Cell inside a CollectionView inside another CollectionView (three layers), resulting in messy and hard-to-maintain code.  
Besides explaining the structure and usage of UICollectionViewCompositionalLayout, the speaker created a project based on this pattern to support the same effect on apps running iOS 12 and earlier — [IBPCollectionViewCompositionalLayout](https://github.com/kishikawakatsumi/IBPCollectionViewCompositionalLayout){:target="_blank"}. Amazing!
translations.去年辦在10月中，我也是去年10月初才開始經營 Medium 記錄生活；結合聽到: Last year it was held in mid-October. I only started managing Medium to record my life in early October last year; combining the UUID topic I heard and my participation experience, I also wrote an [article](../a4bc3bce7513/). This year, I continue to **write my thoughts to ride the hype**!
translations.另外 **關於連線安全的部分** ，前幾天剛好發了一篇「 [APP有用HTTPS:
Regarding **connection security**, a few days ago I posted an article titled "[APP uses HTTPS for transmission, but data was still stolen.](../46410aaada00/)" I used [mitmproxy](https://mitmproxy.org/){:target="_blank"}, a free tool for man-in-the-middle attacks by replacing the root CA. Through the speaker's explanation of man-in-the-middle attacks, their principles, and protection methods, I was able to verify the accuracy of my content and better understand this technique!  
It also opened my eyes... I learned there are jailbreak plugins that can intercept network requests directly without even needing to replace certificates.
translations.吃貨必備，便當照\n\n: A Must-Have for Foodies: Bento Photos
translations.感謝所有工作人員的辛勞及講者的分享，才有這兩天收穫滿滿的活動！\n\n: Thanks to all the staff for their hard work and the speakers for their sharing, which made these two days full of valuable gains!
translations.有任何問題及指教歡迎 [與我聯絡](https://www.zhgchg.li/: If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to [contact me](https://www.zhgchg.li/contact){:target="_blank"}.
translations.沒有保證安全的 APP，只有增加破解的難易度，勸退攻擊者！\n\n: There is no completely secure APP, only increased difficulty in cracking to discourage attackers!
translations.相較 2018 年第一屆，今年在各方面又更大幅度提升！\n\n: Compared to the first event in 2018, this year has seen significant improvements in every aspect!
translations.講者提到的嘗試、經驗分享，很值得回去研究用在自己的專案上！\n\n: The speakers shared their experiments and experiences, which are definitely worth exploring for use in your own projects!
translations.辛苦了！謝謝！\n\n: Thank you for your hard work! Much appreciated!
translations.這也是一直以來苦惱我們的問題，編譯很慢；有時在 UI 微調時真的會抓狂，就只調個: This has always been a frustrating issue for us—slow compilation. Sometimes when fine-tuning the UI, it can be really maddening. You adjust by just 1pt, then wait, see the result, adjust another 1pt, wait again, then change it back... while(true)... very frustrating!
translations.這部分很有感，因為我們的專案並沒有使用第三方網路套件；而是自己封裝方法，講者說的: This part resonated with me because our project does not use third-party networking libraries; instead, we encapsulate methods ourselves. Many design patterns and issues mentioned by the speaker are exactly the optimizations and refactoring we need to do. Applying what the speaker said:
translations.這部分要好好回去研究了，我會做好分類的&lt;( _ _)&gt;\n*p.s:
This part needs a thorough study when I get back. I will organize everything properly <( _ _)>  
*p.s Didn't manage to get the KingFisher sticker QQ*
translations.還有收獲除了 Reveal 這個付費APP之外，還有開源免費的 Lookin 可: Other than the paid app Reveal, there is also the open-source and free Lookin for viewing app UI; we use Reveal frequently. Even if you don't inspect others, it's very convenient for debugging your own UI issues!
translations.關於 **APP 本身的安全問題，** 從未認真研究過，固有觀念就是「蘋果很封閉: Regarding **the security issues of the APP itself,** I had never seriously studied it before, holding the preconceived notion that "Apple is very closed and secure!" After listening to the presentations of two speakers, I realized it is fragile rather than strong, and I understood the core concepts of APP security:
translations.除了技術方面的收穫，我個人比去年更多的是「 **軟性收穫** 」，第一次跟 [E: Besides technical gains, I personally had more **soft gains** than last year. It was my first time meeting [Ethan Huang](https://medium.com/u/e13f6afcf9b9){:target="_blank"}, and while discussing Apple Watch development, I also had a brief chat with the Cat God. I also met many new developers, including my colleague Frank, [George Liu](https://medium.com/u/72361fccaa43){:target="_blank"}'s classmate [Taihsin](https://twitter.com/taihsin_l){:target="_blank"}, [Spock Xue](https://medium.com/u/e55ade4a40a3){:target="_blank"}, [Crystal Liu](https://medium.com/u/2b9530ad5d14){:target="_blank"}, [Nia Fan](https://medium.com/u/8fdb2b5b6672){:target="_blank"}, Alice, Ada, old classmate [Peter Chen](https://medium.com/u/d3a2b0073ab2){:target="_blank"}, former colleague Hao Ge [邱鈺晧](https://medium.com/u/bee7081e8048){:target="_blank"}, and many other new friends!
